---
title: "nitrix.graph.laplacian"
generated: true
signature-hash: c70a260991f9a2cd
description: "Graph Laplacian variants and the shared sparse-matvec machinery."
listing:
  id: nitrix-graph-laplacian-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-graph-laplacian/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.graph.laplacian`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Graph Laplacian variants and the shared sparse-matvec machinery.

This module is intentionally pure "graph Laplacians": the
modularity-related operators live in `community.py` because their
*role* is community detection even though their algebraic shape
overlaps with Laplacians.  The split keeps the Laplacian import
graph free of community-detection concerns.

Three Laplacian variants exposed via `laplacian`
(selected by `normalisation=...`):

- `"combinatorial"` -- $L = D - A$.  Eigenvalues unbounded.
- `"symmetric"` -- $L = I - D^{-1/2} A D^{-1/2}$.  Symmetric,
  positive semidefinite, eigenvalues in $[0, 2]$; the canonical
  "normalised Laplacian" used in Laplacian-eigenmap embeddings.
- `"random_walk"` -- $L = I - D^{-1} A$.  Not symmetric in
  general but shares the spectrum of `"symmetric"` and is the
  generator of the random walk on the graph.

Multi-format support: [`degree_vector`](nitrix-graph-laplacian/degree_vector.qmd) and [`laplacian_matvec`](nitrix-graph-laplacian/laplacian_matvec.qmd)
accept dense `(..., n, n)`, [`ELL`](nitrix-sparse.qmd), or
[`SectionedELL`](nitrix-sparse.qmd) inputs.  The explicit
`laplacian` returns dense -- by definition, since the symmetric
Laplacian's diagonal is generally non-zero and does not fit the ELL
pattern of the input.  For *operator* use (eigenvalue solvers, etc.)
call [`laplacian_matvec`](nitrix-graph-laplacian/laplacian_matvec.qmd), which never materialises the dense matrix.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-graph-laplacian-symbols}
:::
